description_long: This object consists of a set of four hand tools, each featuring a wooden handle and a metal shaft with a flat, chisel-like tip. The handles are cylindrical and show signs of wear, with some areas appearing darker or more worn than others, indicating frequent use. The metal shafts are straight and extend from the handles, with visible tarnishing and discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to elements. The tips are flat and rectangular, characteristic of flathead screwdrivers or chisels, and some have visible nicks or dulling from repeated contact with hard surfaces. The handles vary slightly in shape and size, but all are ergonomically designed for grip and manual force application. No visible brand markings or logos are present on any of the tools.
description: A set of four well-used flathead screwdrivers with wooden handles and tarnished metal shafts, showing visible wear and aging. The handles are cylindrical and ergonomically shaped for grip.
description_view_0: Four screwdrivers are seen from an angled perspective, highlighting the flat metal tips and the rounded, worn wooden handles.
description_view_1: The screwdrivers are viewed from the side, clearly showing the length of the metal shafts and the varying handle shapes.
description_view_2: The tools are viewed from the handle ends, displaying the circular cross-sections and the wear on the wood.
description_view_3: A top-down view reveals the full length of the handles and shafts, with the flat tips visible at the far end.
attribute_handle_type: cylindrical, contoured, wooden, visibly worn, ergonomic
attribute_visible_wear_or_damage: Handles show discoloration, scratches, and worn areas; metal shafts are tarnished and tips are dulled or nicked.
